summary: Relaxed error handling in the Rust `melee-issues` tooling so missing or empty Clang diagnostic strings no longer abort processing. Instead of converting null/empty filename, diagnostic category, or diagnostic spelling into `anyhow!` errors, the tool now interns and uses the placeholder symbol `"<Unknown>"`.
what_changed: In `process_info`, `cxstr_to_symbol(unsafe { clang_getFileName(file) }, str_interner)` now falls back to `str_interner.get_or_intern("<Unknown>")` instead of returning `Err(anyhow!("Null or empty filename."))`.
In `process_info`, `clang_getDiagnosticCategoryText(diagnostic)` conversion now falls back to an interned `"<Unknown>"` symbol instead of erroring on a null or empty category.
In `process_info`, `clang_getDiagnosticSpelling(diagnostic)` conversion now falls back to an interned `"<Unknown>"` symbol instead of erroring on a null or empty spelling.
The PR changed one file with a small 3-line replacement pattern: `.ok_or(anyhow!(...))?` became `.unwrap_or(str_interner.get_or_intern("<Unknown>"))` for non-critical diagnostic text fields.
smart_moves: Preserved the existing `Symbol`/string interner flow by using `str_interner.get_or_intern("<Unknown>")` rather than introducing a separate optional-field path.
Made diagnostic collection more tolerant of incomplete libclang data while keeping a visible placeholder in reports.
Applied the same fallback consistently to filename, category, and spelling fields, reducing special-case behavior in `DiagnosticInfo` construction.
decomp_lessons: For project tooling that summarizes compiler or Clang diagnostics, absent metadata should not necessarily be fatal; a stable placeholder can allow the rest of the report to be generated.
When a tool uses interned strings, fallback values should be interned through the same mechanism as normal values to preserve type and storage expectations.
Small robustness fixes in supporting tools can unblock decomp workflows even when they do not touch game code or matching output.
naming_conventions: Uses the placeholder string `"<Unknown>"` for missing filename, diagnostic category, and diagnostic spelling text.
Keeps existing Rust helper naming such as `process_info`, `cxstr_to_symbol`, and `str_interner` unchanged.
follow_up_queries: Where does `DiagnosticInfo` consume filename/category/spelling, and should those fields be explicitly documented as possibly `"<Unknown>"`?
Are there other `cxstr_to_symbol(...).ok_or(anyhow!(...))?` call sites in `tools/issues` that should use the same non-fatal fallback policy?
Should the placeholder spelling be standardized across other tooling as `"<Unknown>"` versus lowercase or empty-string defaults?
